titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Irene Sharaff Papers
titlestmt.author: by Rosemary K. J. Davis and Michael Rush

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday May 23, 2019 at 03:02

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: These materials are open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Transferred from the Yale School of Drama, 2018.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four series: I. Personal Papers, 1934-1992. II. Production Files, 1941-1989. III. Research Files, 1941-1991. IV. Designs and Artwork, 1945-2014.
Contains some legacy information written on the housing, including folder and box numbers from previous processing work performed on the collection.

bioghist.head: Irene Sharaff
bioghist.p: Irene Sharaff was an American costume designer for both the stage and screen. Born in 1910 in Boston, Sharaff studied art and design in Boston, New York, and Paris. She won five Academy Awards for her costume designs, including awards for An American in Paris (1951), West Side Story (1961), and The King and I (1956). She was also awarded a 1952 Tony Award for The King and I . Sharaff died in 1993.

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: Bequest of Irene Sharaff to the Yale School of Drama, 1993.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Irene Sharaff Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Initial arrangement and description of the Irene Sharaff Papers was performed by Amanda Walker and Susan Brady for the Yale School of Drama Design Department, 2006.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Irene Sharaff Papers, which span from 1934 to 2014, contain artwork, research notes, clippings, and other documentation related to her life and work. Includes drawings and sketches related to Sharaff's most famous work as a costume designer including An American in Paris , The King and I , West Side Story , and Who's Afraid of Virginia Woolf? .

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Irene Sharaff Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

subject.part: Costume designers -- United States -- 20th Century -- Archives

appraisal.head: Appraisal
appraisal.p: Materials within this series are organized into six subseries: Correspondence, Datebooks and documents, Photographs and clippings, Writings and ephemera, Mai-Mai Sze personal files, and Magazines, programs, and artworks.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence, photographs, clippings, writings, and other documents related to Sharaff's life.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this subseries are arranged alphabetically.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials within this subseries are arranged chronologically, with undated materials placed at the end.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials within this subseries reflect archival arrangement performed prior to acquisition.

did.unittitle: House and Garden, "Color" by Irene Sharaff

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials within this subseries reflect archival arrangement performed prior to acquisition.

did.unittitle: Broadway and Hollywood book--text blocks, captions
Broadway and Hollywood book--rights and permissions

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials within this subseries reflect archival arrangement performed prior to acquisition.

did.unittitle: The Mustard Seed Garden Manual of Painting edited by Mai-Mai Sze
Sample pages for The Tao of Painting

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials within this subseries reflect archival arrangement performed prior to acquisition.

did.unittitle: Production programs for productions not designed by Irene Sharaff

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials within this series are organized into three subseries: Theatre, Dance, and Film.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains research notes, photographs, clippings, writings, and other documents related to Sharaff's work.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials within this subseries are mostly arranged alphabetically by production title, with some oversized materials placed at the end.

King and I two swatch books [remount]
King and I two swatch books [remount]

Programs, Can-Can and Taming of the Shrew

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials within this subseries reflect archival arrangement performed prior to acquisition.

did.unittitle: Afternoon of a Faun Australian Ballet program
Jerome Robbins' Broadway program, autographed photo
Pam T. G. G. sketches and programs
Jack American Dance Machine, Costume bible

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials within this series are mostly arranged alphabetically by production title, with some oversized material placed at the end.

did.unittitle: Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences Nominations and certificate as voting member

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes nominations for An American in Paris , Call Me Madame , and A Star is Born .

did.unittitle: Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences Nominations

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes nominations for Brigadoon , Guys and Dolls , The King and I , and Porgy and Bess .

did.unittitle: Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences Nominations

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes nominations for Can Can , West Side Story , Flower Drum Song , and Cleopatra .

did.unittitle: Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences Nominations

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes nominations for Who's Afraid of Virginia Woolf? , Taming of the Shrew , Helly Dolly! , and The Other Side of Midnight .

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials within this series reflect archival arrangement performed prior to acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains postcards, photographs, clippings, and other ephermera related to Sharaff's work-related research.

did.unittitle: Seventeenth and eighteenth century fashion plates
Native Americans and United States postcards

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Clippings remain housed in original Brooks Brothers box as kept by Irene Sharaff.

did.unittitle: King and Queen weddings clipping files

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials within this series are arranged into four subseries: Film, Theatre, Dance, and Artwork.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains drawings, artwork, notes, and other items related to Sharaff's costume designs for stage and screen.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials within this subseries are arranged alphabetically with unidentified materials placed at the end.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials within this subseries are arranged alphabetically.

did.unittitle: The Girl Who Came to Supper

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials within this subseries are arranged alphabetically.
